essence: Designs and integrates complete CNC‑based manufacturing systems, combining machines, robotics, sensors, and software into unified production environments.

identity.title: CNC Systems Engineer
identity.summary: Develops and integrates CNC systems, robotics, automation, sensors, and control software to create efficient, reliable, and scalable manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Systems engineers in aerospace, defence, or smart‑factory environments can exceed £120k-£160k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC automation, robotics, or manufacturing engineering
Knowledge of industrial control systems, PLCs, sensors, and networking
Ability to design integrated CNC‑robotic workflows
Understanding of software, data systems, and Industry 4.0 technologies

lifestyle.description: CNC systems engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, robotics, and smart‑factory environments. They design integrated systems that combine CNC machines, robots, sensors, and software into cohesive production ecosystems. The role suits individuals who enjoy systems thinking, automation, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Implements sensors, PLCs, and industrial networks
Optimises workflow, automation, and data flow
Collaborates with robotics, CNC, and software teams
Ensures reliability, safety, and precision across systems
